title: Who won the Bax vs Arcon ATP Challenger tennis match on May 5, 2026?
rules: If Florent Bax wins the Bax vs Arcon professional tennis match in the 2026 ATP Challenger Brazzaville Round Of 32 after a ball has been played, then the market resolves to Yes.
tavily_brief: [redacted] The two players have no prior head-to-head record. **Florent Bax Profile:** Bax, 26, is seeded #1 at Brazzaville and ranked world No. 254 as of early May 2026 (per TennisLive.net). He has compiled a strong 20-11 record in 2026 with an 8-4 clay record, demonstrating particular comfort on this surface. His career clay record stands at 172-93 overall. Bax reached the final of the M25 Santa Margherita di Pula earlier in 2026. He lost in the quarterfinals of the Abidjan 2 Challenger on May 1 to Niels Visker (7-6, 6-3), suggesting he arrives at Brazzaville with recent match play but a fresh loss. **Adrian Arcon Profile:** Arcon, 24, is an Australian qualifier with a 13-10 record in 2026 but only 1-0 on clay this year. His career record shows 45% win rate (94-208 matches). He qualified for Brazzaville by defeating Medhir Goyal 6-0, 6-1 on May 3, displaying dominant serving (12 aces, 83% first-serve points won, 93% second-serve points won). However, his overall clay record is weak at 9-15 over six years, and he remains unranked. **Market Signals:** Prediction markets show overwhelming confidence in Bax. Polymarket prices Bax at 94% implied probability versus Arcon at 7% (per Polymarket). Tennis Tonic's expert prediction favors Bax in two sets. Betting odds show Bax at 1.005 versus Arcon at 10.75. **Key Factors:** Bax's #1 seeding, superior ranking (254 vs. unranked), strong 2026 form, and clay expertise heavily favor him. Arcon's main asset is his powerful serve, demonstrated in qualifying, but his weak career clay record and limited experience at this level work against him. The 20-11 vs. 13-10 2026 records further favor Bax. **Most important facts:** (1) Florent Bax is the tournament's top seed with a 254 ATP ranking and 8-4 clay record in 2026, while Adrian Arcon is an unranked qualifier with only 1-0 on clay this year. (2) Prediction markets price Bax at 94% probability, reflecting his substantial advantage in experience, ranking, and surface suitability. (3) This is their first career meeting, with no prior head-to-head data to inform the forecast.

args.reasoning:
Evidence inventory:
- Florent Bax (FRA, b. 1999): Career-high ATP singles ranking of #254 achieved on 4 May 2026 (the day before this match), per Wikipedia. This is an established Challenger-level player at or near his career peak form.
- Adrian Arcon (AUS, b. 2001): Per Tennis Abstract (pre-cutoff April 2026), current rank #2226, peak rank #2211. This is essentially an ITF/futures-level player, roughly 2000 ranking spots below Bax. The 1110 figure appears to be a doubles ranking.
- The match is ATP Challenger Brazzaville R32, May 5, 2026. Bax is at career-high form on a clay/hard Challenger; Arcon is far below Challenger main-draw level (likely a qualifier or wildcard).

Implication: A ~#254 ranked player at career peak versus a ~#2226 ranked player represents an enormous skill gap on the ATP Challenger tour. Historical ATP/Challenger data suggests that when a top-300 player faces an opponent outside the top-1500, the favorite wins roughly 85-92% of the time. The 2000-spot gap is much larger than typical Challenger R32 mismatches.

Kalshi opening prices (Bax 0.695 / Arcon 0.445) sum to 1.14, implying significant vig/uncertainty but Bax as clear favorite. De-vigged, Bax is around 0.61. However, the ranking disparity I found suggests the market is underpricing Bax considerably — possibly because Arcon's name/nationality isn't well-known and openings are noisy. The fundamentals strongly favor Bax.

Upset check: No injury, scandal, or withdrawal news found for Bax. He just hit career-high ranking the day before, suggesting good form. No reason to fade him.

Sanity: Base rate for ~2000-spot ranking gap at Challenger level ≈ 85-90% for favorite. I'll set Bax at 0.87, slightly above the de-vigged market because the ranking gap is unusually large and I have specific confirmation of Bax's career-high form.
